      "それは祈りを込めて組まれた手で描かれていることが特徴です。",
      "それは魂のこもった目で描かれていることが特徴です。",
      "それは奇跡的な癒しが特徴です。",
      "それは戦争中に通行不能な沼地の隠し場所に隠されていたことが特徴です。",
      "それは何度も強盗に盗まれたことが特徴です。",
      "それはヴォルィーニのイコン博物館で修復されたことが特徴です。"

About: It is an icon which allegedly appeared in the 18th century and honored as miraculous by the Orthodox Christians of Volyn, Rivne (Ukraine), and Pinsk (Belarus) Polesye.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Orthodox Christians of Volyn, Rivne (Ukraine), and Pinsk (Belarus) Polesye.
It is associated with the St. Nicholas Church, Ukrainian Orthodox Church of the village of Buchyn.
It is associated with the village of Buchyn in the Lyubeshiv district of Volyn oblast of Ukraine.
It is associated with a 15-kilometer long religious procession that takes place annually on May 22.
It is associated with the village of Zarudchi in the Lyubeshiv district.
It is associated with the river Stokhid.
It is associated with the Russian Uniate Church – today Ukrainian Greek Catholic Church.
It is associated with the Minsk diocese of the Russian Orthodox Church.
It is associated with the Polessie diocese of the Polish Orthodox Church.
It is associated with the Volyn-Rivne Diocese of the Russian Orthodox Church.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Volyn Eparchy of the Ukrainian Orthodox Church of the Moscow Patriarchate since 1990.
BuiltIn: It was built in the village of Buchyn.
CreatedAround: It was created around the 18th century.
DiscoveredAt: It was discovered at the island among the floodplain of the river Stokhid.
DiscoveredIn: It was discovered in the spring of 1706.
HasA: It has a size of 65x80 cm.
It has hands folded in prayer.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being honored as miraculous by Orthodox Christians.
It is characterized by a religious procession that takes place annually.
It is characterized by being depicted with hands folded in prayer.
It is characterized by being depicted with soulful eyes.
It is characterized by miraculous healing.
It is characterized by being secreted in a hiding place amid impassable swamps during wars.
It is characterized by being stolen by robbers many times.
It is characterized by being restored at the Museum of Volyn Icons in Lutsk.
HeldIn: It was held in the St. Nicholas Church.
It was held in the church of Derevok village of the Lyubeshiv district.
It was held in the Temple of Buchyn village.
IsA: It is a Blessed Virgin depiction.
KnownAs: It is known as Our Lady of Buchyn.
It is known as Buchynska Theotokos.
KnownFor: It is known for being honored as miraculous.
It is known for miraculous healing.
It is known for being stolen and returned many times.
LocatedIn: It is located in the village of Buchyn in the Lyubeshiv district of Volyn oblast of Ukraine.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the First and Second World Wars.
It occurred during Divine Services termination in the Temple of Buchyn from 1961 to 1990.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the spring of 1706.
It occurred in the Lyubeshiv district.
It occurred in Volyn oblast of Ukraine.
It occurred in the village of Derevok.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the village of Buchyn.
PartOf: It is part of the St. Nicholas Church.
It is part of the Ukrainian Orthodox Church of the village of Buchyn.
It is part of the Volyn Eparchy of the Ukrainian Orthodox Church of the Moscow Patriarchate.
UsedAs: It is used as a religious icon.
UsedFor: It is used for miraculous healing.
UsedIn: It is used in the St. Nicholas Church.
It is used in the Ukrainian Orthodox Church of the village of Buchyn.
It is used in the church of Derevok village of the Lyubeshiv district.
It is used in the Temple of Buchyn village.
